arts.ttl: MARTIN KEOWN TALKS TACTICS: Andoni Iraola's men are ravenous - Bournemouth pin you into submission, as Nottingham Forest will find out
arts.sum: MARTIN KEOWN TALKS TACTICS: Only leaders Liverpool have a better away record than Forest but trips do not come much tougher than a day out at the Vitality Stadium in Bournemouth.

arts.ttl: Ola Aina shows how fine margins are keeping Forest in the top four
arts.sum: In fact, Everton are the only team whose players have earned more points through goalline clearances in the Premier League this season (six) than Aina. As Nottingham Forest fly high in the league, Ali Tweedale of Opta Analyst takes a closer look at how Ola Aina's pivotal performances have played a key role in their incredible form this season.

arts.ttl: The Big Question: How did Bournemouth v Forest become a top-four battle?
arts.sum: Football writer Ben Bloom compares the contrasting styles that have taken two former Championship clubs into the Premier League's top seven. Bournemouth have not tasted defeat for 10 league matches and find themselves only one point behind fifth place, a position that looks highly likely to bring Champions League football next campaign.

Bournemouth extended their unbeaten run to 10 games with a fine 4-1 win at Newcastle last weekend. That streak will be put to the test when they welcome Nottingham Forest to the Vitality Stadium on Sa...

arts.ttl: Two clubs make bids for Nottingham Forest summer signing - journalist
Andoni Iraola provides Marcus Tavernier injury update ahead of Bournemouth's clash with Nottingham Forest
arts.sum: Bournemouth head coach Andoni Iraola provides an update on Marcus Tavernier's injury recovery ahead of Saturday's Premier League clash with Nottingham Forest.

arts.ttl: ‘Ridiculous’: Andoni Iraola shares what has shocked him about Nottingham Forest this season
Nottingham Forest man shuts down potential exit – Agents had been talking to club for weeks
arts.sum: Brazilian side Vasco have made an attempt to sign Ramón Sosa from Nottingham Forest but received an initial rejection from the player. That’s according to GE Globo, who report that Vasco were willing to spend to secure the Nottingham Forest winger’s signature. They explain that there was a meeting between Sosa and his representatives on The post Nottingham Forest man shuts down potential exit – Agents had been talking to club for weeks appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: ‘I’ve always been pretty chilled’: Dean Huijsen on Bournemouth, defending and his celebration
arts.sum: The teenager on his calm demeanour, learning the defender’s art in Italy and his side’s European credentials He is surely one of the most exciting prospects in the game – José Mourinho said as much around this time last year – but to many Dean Huijsen is just a Chill Guy. Last month the 19-year-old celebrated his first Bournemouth goal, against Tottenham, by mimicking the popular meme of a human-like dog smirking with paws in pockets, and then did the same a couple of weeks later after opening the scoring in a resounding win at Old Trafford. A TikTok clip of Huijsen imitating the image, posted by Bournemouth, has been viewed 11m times. “I’ve always been a pretty relaxed person, pretty chilled,” Huijsen says. “I think I am pretty calm on the ball, I make my decisions and I don’t get too panicked. I thought it would be a nice celebration but didn’t expect it to go so viral. I just thought it would be funny –I guess people liked it.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Embracing the chaos - breaking down Bournemouth's rise
arts.sum: With Bournemouth challenging for a top-four place in the Premier League this season, BBC Sport analyses how the Cherries are doing it.
